name: Single therapy instance with natural compound mapping — structurally typed and Frobenius-verified therapy design output, linked to specific Phytoglyphica/Fungiglyphica/Animaglyphica compounds via routing morphisms

phase_0.domain_name: Single therapy instance with natural compound mapping — structurally typed and Frobenius-verified therapy design output, linked to specific Phytoglyphica/Fungiglyphica/Animaglyphica compounds via routing morphisms
phase_0.boundary_condition: therapeutic efficacy: the closed loop of disease diagnosis, compound selection, and structural verification that returns a verified therapy design

VINIT.justification: the void/uninitialized state before any therapeutic intervention is applied; the raw disease tuple is the seed

TANCH.justification: the terminal anchor that contains the entire therapy system; the healthy state is the boundary toward which all operations converge

AFWD.justification: forward transformation that lifts a primitive from a lower to a higher ordinal value (e.g., φ̂: 𐑢 → ⊙); the primary therapeutic direction

AREV.justification: reverse/descent transformation that lowers a primitive from a higher to a lower ordinal value (e.g., φ̂: 𐑣 → ⊙); the conservative floor operation

CLINK.justification: composition of multiple therapeutic steps into a single chain; the agglutination of tensor and meet operations in temporal order

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: structural identity (self-recognition of the therapy as its own type)
IMSCRIB.justification: the therapy design recognizes itself as a valid structural type; the compound's tuple is self-consistent and Frobenius-verified

FSPLIT.chosen_element: dual-component therapy decision (split into two arms)
FSPLIT.justification: the therapy branches into two distinct components: one arm for φ̂ correction (criticality) and one arm for Ħ correction (chirality); each arm maps to a specific compound from the glyphica catalogs

FFUSE.chosen_element: combined therapy protocol (reconstitution of the two arms)
FFUSE.justification: the two components are administered in sequence (or combination) to reconstitute the original disease state as a corrected health state; FFUSE(FSPLIT(disease)) = health

EVALT.chosen_element: successful primitive correction (e.g., φ̂ reaches ⊙, Ħ reaches 𐑖)
EVALT.justification: affirmative state: the target primitive has been promoted/demoted to the health value; the therapy component is effective

EVALF.chosen_element: failed primitive correction (primitive remains at disease value or overshoots)
EVALF.justification: negative state: the therapy component did not achieve the target primitive value; the compound is ineffective or counterproductive

ENGAGR.chosen_element: paradice: both φ̂ and Ħ are simultaneously held in their target states despite requiring opposite operations (tensor vs meet)
ENGAGR.justification: a paradice: the therapy must simultaneously promote Ħ (via tensor) and demote φ̂ (via meet) — two operations that cannot be performed by a single compound; the dual-component protocol holds both without resolution until the sequential protocol resolves them

IFIX.chosen_element: therapy design record (the final verified tuple, compound SMILES, and Lean verification theorem)
IFIX.justification: permanent irreversible fixation: once the therapy design is structurally verified and Frobenius-closed, it is recorded as a canonical entry in the therapy catalog; append-only, cannot be undone

split_outputs: φ̂-correction arm: targets criticality (e.g., meet with ⊙-stabilizer from phytoglyphica)
Ħ-correction arm: targets chirality (e.g., tensor with NMDA-PAM from fungiglyphica)

phase_3.void_description: 00 — uninitialized disease state before any therapy design begins; no compounds selected, no structural analysis performed
phase_3.true_description: 01 — affirmative: the therapy design is structurally verified and Frobenius-closed; the compound tuple matches the target health tuple
phase_3.false_description: 10 — negative: the therapy design fails structural verification; the compound tuple does not achieve the required primitive corrections
phase_3.both_description: 11 — paradice: the therapy design is structurally valid but requires two opposite operations (tensor and meet) that cannot be performed by a single compound; the dual-component protocol holds both arms simultaneously without resolution until the sequential protocol is executed

steps.domain_action: disease state is identified and imscribed as a 12-primitive tuple (e.g., schizophrenia, HIV, gout)
health state is defined as the target tuple (e.g., healthy brain, normal immune, normal urate)
the therapy design splits into two arms — one for φ̂ correction (criticality) and one for Ħ correction (chirality)
tensor operation is applied to the Ħ-correction arm: the compound from fungiglyphica (e.g., NMDA-PAM) promotes Ħ from 𐑒 to 𐑖
the Ħ-correction arm is evaluated: if Ħ reaches 𐑖, the arm is successful (EVALT); if not, the arm fails (EVALF)
meet operation is applied to the φ̂-correction arm: the compound from phytoglyphica (e.g., ⊙-stabilizer) demotes φ̂ from 𐑣 to ⊙
the φ̂-correction arm is evaluated: if φ̂ reaches ⊙, the arm is successful (EVALT); if not, the arm fails (EVALF)
both arms are held simultaneously — the therapy design must accommodate both tensor and meet operations, a paradice that is resolved by sequential administration
the two arms are fused into a combined therapy protocol: the tensor and meet operations are sequenced (e.g., tensor first, then meet) to reconstitute the health state
the therapy design recognizes itself as a valid structural type: the combined tuple matches the health tuple, and the Frobenius condition μ∘δ=id is verified
the sequential therapy protocol is composed: phase 1 (tensor) → phase 2 (meet) → maintenance; the chain is closed
the therapy design is permanently recorded: the compound SMILES, structural tuple, and Lean verification theorem are appended to the therapy catalog; this record is irreversible and append-only

phase_5.compiler_frontend: ch3mpiler retrosynthetic compiler: translates the target structural tuple into a molecular design by performing retrosynthetic bond disconnection, ranking disconnections by Frobenius distance
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: serpent_rod pipeline: communicates between the structural type system and the protein/DARPin design modules via Frobenius-exact complementarity mapping
phase_5.memory_mechanism: crystal of types (17,280,000-type lattice): stores all possible structural tuples and their addresses; therapy designs are indexed by their crystal address
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: ouroboric pill simulator: determines the order and timing of compound administration (tensor first, then meet) based on biomarker feedback and Frobenius kernel closure
phase_5.alfs_store: Lean 4 formal verification modules (Core.lean, Frobenius.lean, AgentSelf.lean): the canonical bootstrap store for all structural claims; every therapy design must pass Lean verification before fixation

phase_6.cycle_cost: one Frobenius verification cycle per therapy design (computational cost of Lean proof checking and crystal address computation)
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: disease state tuple (unverified, unclosed — the Frobenius loop is open)
phase_6.post_cycle_state: health state tuple (verified, Frobenius-closed — μ∘δ=id holds, the therapy design is structurally complete)
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the Frobenius condition μ∘δ=id ensures that the therapy design is entropy-conservative: the information content of the disease state is exactly reconstituted as the health state through the split and fuse operations; no information is lost or created
